findings.title: `swapExactJarForJar()` trusts attacker-supplied jars and converter calldata, exposing controller-held tokens to theft

best_agent_match.title: Public jar swaps delegatecall user-supplied whitelisted helpers, exposing controller-held tokens to sweep/approval gadgets

findings.title: Permissionless harvests are sandwichable because reward swaps and reinvestment use zero minimums

best_agent_match.title: Permissionless harvests are sandwichable because reward swaps and reinvestment use zero slippage

best_agent_match.title: Public LP conversion contracts expose user principal to MEV because every leg uses zero minimums

findings.title: `SCRVVoter.deposit()` is permissionless and can route voter-controlled tokens into an attacker-chosen gauge

best_agent_match.title: `SCRVVoter.deposit` is permissionless and can route voter-controlled assets into attacker-chosen gauges

findings.title: The timelock delay can be bypassed for the first admin handoff

best_agent_match.title: The timelock can be bypassed for the first admin handoff

rejected_candidates.title: Unrestricted Strategic Execution via Delegatecall
rejected_candidates.reason: `StrategyBase.execute()` is restricted to `timelock` and serves as an explicit privileged emergency hook; this is a trust/governance capability, not a permissionless vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Centralization Risk - Governance Can Change Critical Addresses
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a generic governance-trust observation rather than a distinct code vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Deprecated now Keyword Usage
rejected_candidates.reason: Use of `now`/short deadlines is a style or UX concern here, not a concrete protocol exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: Insufficient Access Control on Keeper Functions
rejected_candidates.reason: `leverageUntil()` and `deleverageUntil()` are restricted to governance/strategist-approved keepers; the reported risk depends on already trusting a privileged role to whitelist an attacker.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Zero Address Validation
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an admin-misconfiguration concern, not a realistic permissionless exploit.

rejected_candidates.title: Approval Race Condition
rejected_candidates.reason: The cited approve-to-zero-then-set pattern is the standard mitigation, not an exploitable race in this context.

rejected_candidates.title: Unlimited Token Approvals
rejected_candidates.reason: Approving fixed, well-known protocol routers is an accepted trust assumption here and no contract-specific exploit path was shown.

rejected_candidates.title: Potential Division Before Multiplication in MasterChef
rejected_candidates.reason: This is ordinary integer rounding in reward accounting and not a material vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: OnlyBenevolent Modifier Allows tx.origin
rejected_candidates.reason: The meaningful security consequence is the public-harvest MEV issue already captured in F-002; `tx.origin` itself is not a separate reportable exploit here.

rejected_candidates.title: No Pausable Protection on Critical Operations
rejected_candidates.reason: Allowing withdrawals while paused is typically an intentional emergency design choice, not a vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing Reentrancy Protection in PickleJar Withdraw
rejected_candidates.reason: `PickleJar.withdraw()` burns shares before transferring assets, so the claimed stale-state reentrancy path is unsupported.

rejected_candidates.title: Timelock Execute Allows Arbitrary Value Transfer
rejected_candidates.reason: This is the intended behavior of a timelock-admin executor and not a permissionless bug.

rejected_candidates.title: CRVLocker Execute Allows Arbitrary Calls
rejected_candidates.reason: `CRVLocker.execute()` is limited to approved voters or governance; this is a privileged-role trust assumption.

rejected_candidates.title: SCRVVoter Allows Strategy to Execute Arbitrary Calls
rejected_candidates.reason: Approved strategies are trusted actors by design; the actual reportable issue is the missing authorization on `SCRVVoter.deposit()`, captured in F-004.

rejected_candidates.title: Unsafe Casting in Strategy Curve 3CRV
rejected_candidates.reason: `getMostPremium()` only returns indices 0, 1, or 2, so the claimed out-of-bounds array access is not supported by the code.

rejected_candidates.title: Hardcoded Gas Stipend May Cause Execution Failure
rejected_candidates.reason: `delegatecall(sub(gas(), 5000), ...)` forwards nearly all remaining gas to the callee; it does not cap the callee at 5000 gas.

rejected_candidates.title: MasterChef Dev Fund Rate Can Be Set to Any Value
rejected_candidates.reason: This is an owner/governance policy choice rather than a permissionless contract flaw.

rejected_candidates.title: PickleJar Ratio Manipulation Via Deposit Order
rejected_candidates.reason: The cited deposit share accounting follows standard vault math and the proposed sandwich theft path is not substantiated by the implementation.
